reason_for_decision: The revised article now clears the pass threshold with stronger consequence framing, clearer security-resilience integration, and improved workflow focus.
strengths: The article now starts from consequence and dependency, which is the right organizing logic for critical-infrastructure protection.
It connects surveillance architecture to escalation, continuity, and operator ownership instead of treating security as a device catalog.
The added exercise and workflow language improves realism and lowers the risk of the piece reading like a generic template.
summary_assessment: This version passes because it is more specific, more operationally grounded, and less generic than the earlier draft. It now behaves like a real system-design article rather than a high-level checklist.
